seo.title: LED Video Wall Rentals — BEATROX
seo.description: Beatrox provides LED video wall rentals for events, concerts, activations, and installations — including panel sourcing, configuration, media server integration, and on-site LED technician support.

og.title: LED Video Wall Rentals — BEATROX Portland
og.description: High-resolution LED panels for any footprint — with full media server integration, custom content, and on-site technical support from Beatrox.

hero.headline: LED Video Wall Rentals
hero.subheadline: High-resolution LED panels for any footprint — with full media server integration, custom content, and on-site technical support.

body.content: From intimate brand activations to massive festival stages, Beatrox delivers LED video wall systems that fit your space, your content, and your budget. Our team handles everything from panel selection and structural integration to media server programming and live operation.

body.heading: Why Choose Beatrox
items: 15+ years of experience in live event production
Full-service team from design to strike
Trusted by Adidas, Netflix, CNN, Amazon Music

body.heading: Our Process
items: 1. Discovery Call — We learn your vision, venue, and budget
2. Design & Pre-Visualization — See your event before it happens
3. Equipment & Crew — We source everything and assign the right team
4. On-Site Execution — Professional setup, operation, and strike
5. Post-Event Review — We ensure everything exceeded expectations

body.heading: Turnkey LED Solutions
body.content: We source, configure, and operate LED video wall systems of any size and resolution. Our team has extensive experience with seamless wall-to-wall builds in complex environments, including custom infinity mirror configurations and non-standard architectural surfaces. Every rental includes on-site LED technician support.

body.heading: Common Event Sizes & Pixel Pitch Guide
body.content: Choosing the right pixel pitch ensures your content looks crisp from every seat. For intimate spaces and close-viewing activations, 1.5mm–2.5mm delivers broadcast-quality resolution. Mid-range viewing (10–30ft) works best with 2.9mm–3.9mm. For outdoor festivals and large venues where viewers are 50ft+ away, 6mm–10mm provides excellent brightness and cost efficiency. Our team helps you match the right panel to your venue and audience.

body.heading: Content Formatting Tips
body.content: Delivering content that looks stunning on LED requires the right formats and workflow. We recommend ProRes 422 or high-bitrate H.264 for video playback. Content should be rendered at the exact pixel dimensions of your wall configuration to avoid scaling artifacts. For live VJ performances, we integrate with Resolume, TouchDesigner, and proprietary media servers, ensuring frame-synced playback across multi-panel builds. Our team handles pixel mapping, color calibration, and content testing before your event.

body.heading: Common Specifications
items: ROE, Absen, and Unilumin panel options
Touring-grade road cases and rigging hardware

items.question: How far in advance should I book?
items.answer: We recommend booking at least 4–6 weeks in advance for standard rentals and 8–12 weeks for custom productions. Last-minute requests are often accommodated depending on availability.

items.question: Do you travel outside Portland?
items.answer: Absolutely. While we're headquartered in Portland, OR, we regularly serve clients across the West Coast and nationwide for touring and large-scale events.

items.question: What's included in the rental?
items.answer: Every rental includes delivery, setup, on-site technical support, and strike. Custom packages can also include content design, pre-visualization, and dedicated project management.

items.question: Can you build custom shapes and configurations?
items.answer: Yes. We specialize in non-standard LED builds including curved walls, columns, infinity mirrors, and architectural integration. Our design team creates pre-visualizations before fabrication.

items.question: What content formats do you support?
items.answer: We support ProRes, H.264, HAP, and Notch for video. For live VJ workflows, we integrate Resolume, TouchDesigner, and proprietary media servers with frame-synced output.
